segments.integrated_caption: The video opens with a black screen displaying white text: 'POV - You bought a ticket to Corgi-Cafe in Bangkok,' immediately accompanied by muffled speech and a dog bark, setting an immersive and playful tone. The scene transitions to the interior of the Corgi-Cafe, where a line of fluffy, tan-and-white corgis with perky ears and colorful collars are lying in a neat row along a concrete ledge. A man in a black t-shirt, black pants, and beige sandals approaches them, bending down to gently pet each dog in turn, as excited human voices exclaim "Hi guys!" and dogs bark and yip playfully. He crouches to interact more closely, smiling as he strokes their heads and backs, with the dogs appearing calm and receptive, some lifting their heads to nuzzle his hand amidst the barks. The cafe has a modern, minimalist design with concrete pillars, wooden flooring, and a decorative white lattice wall. In the background, shelves display merchandise, and a sign indicates 'SNACK 100 BAHT.' As the man finishes petting the corgis, he stands and walks away, and the camera pans to reveal a wider view of the cafe. Several patrons are seated at tables, some wearing blue booties, interacting with corgis that roam freely, contributing to the general background chatter and frequent dog barks. A child's voice is distinctly heard, adding to the lively ambiance. One man in a white shirt pets a corgi on his lap, while a woman in a light blue dress sits cross-legged, observing. The atmosphere is relaxed and joyful, with people and dogs coexisting in a cozy, pet-friendly environment, filled with the sounds of human interaction and happy dog vocalizations.

segments.integrated_caption: The video captures a lively and warm scene inside a modern corgi-themed cafe, filled with the sounds of indistinct chatter, occasional dog barks, and playful 'Woof!' sounds. A 'SNACK 100 BAHT' sign is visible. A Man in black outfit, wearing a corgi-graphic t-shirt, gently pets and crouches beside the tan-and-white corgis, later standing and gesturing. He is heard guiding patrons with phrases like "Okay, I'll show you first. Snack, I'll show you first," and later instructing, "We can move here," and "You can keep it." He also interacts directly with the dogs, calling out "Good boy, good boy" and mimicking their barks with "Woof, woof, woof, woof, woof." The man in black outfit continues to guide, advising, "You can give it to her. She's a good girl," and "the snack you can put on your palm." A Man in a gray hoodie and face mask enters from the right, holding a yellow cup, and moves through the space, briefly interacting with the corgis. Towards the end, a female voice is briefly heard amidst the ongoing cafe sounds, saying, "I think for you first. Sorry, but..." The atmosphere remains cheerful and playful, with corgis energetically seeking attention and people responding with affection.

segments.integrated_caption: The video opens with a lively scene inside a modern cafe, featuring wooden flooring and black metal stools, where a group of tan-and-white corgis, some wearing colorful collars and bandanas (one with 'Bone' and a rainbow design, another with a tag reading 'Buttercup'), are actively engaging with a woman in a blue shirt and beige pants who is kneeling on the floor. The air is filled with excited corgi barks as a calm female instructor's voice, speaking in Thai, guides the patrons. She praises the dogs with phrases like "good, good" and "very good," and instructs people to let the dogs lick their hands when placed near their mouths. She also issues warnings, advising against throwing food or extending hands directly, explaining that the dogs might bite. As the camera pans, more corgis are seen roaming freely, interacting with other cafe patrons, including a man in a white shirt with a corgi-printed design and white pants, and another man in a black t-shirt with red trim and blue jeans, both wearing blue booties. The instructor's voice continues, repeatedly telling the dogs to "sit down," an instruction echoed by a male patron. The atmosphere is bright and cheerful, with the dogs moving energetically around the space, their barks and the instructor's guidance creating a dynamic and joyful, dog-friendly environment. The camera then shifts focus to a woman in a white shirt and black shorts, sitting cross-legged on the floor, also feeding and petting the corgis, one of which rests its paws on her lap. A black-and-white corgi briefly appears, walking through the scene before lying down to be petted by a new woman with curly hair in a white crop top and white shorts, who kneels to interact with it gently. The cafe’s interior remains consistent with glass doors and a visible 'SNACK 100 BAHT' sign, reflecting the lively environment, punctuated by continuous sounds of happy dogs and human interaction.

segments.integrated_caption: The video opens in a bright, modern cafe with wooden floors, where a black-and-white corgi, wearing a tan bandana, calmly lies on the floor amidst the constant background chatter and playful barks of other dogs. A woman’s hand, adorned with a black bracelet, extends a treat, which the corgi gently takes before standing and trotting away. The camera follows the dog through the lively cafe, where patrons in blue booties sit on black metal stools, some engaging with other corgis, their barks, yelps, and occasional howls echoing through the space. A male voice is heard explaining, "So this is an instruction for people who want to come and see the corgis," as the scene shifts to the entrance, revealing a glass door with a 'Corgi Slide' sign. A woman in a white shirt and black shorts is seen lying on the floor, petting a row of tan-and-white corgis lined up against the wall, while the male voice continues to interact with the dogs, saying "What do you want, boy?" and "Come on, come on, come on."
